skill.name: context7
skill.description: Fetch current library documentation from the Context7 API for framework, package, and API implementation work.
skill.summary: Retrieves current Context7 documentation for third-party libraries during coding tasks.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, environment, filesystem, and command-execution indicators. AI review confirms real external API calls and a concerning instruction to search hidden .env files for CONTEXT7_API_KEY; markdown command examples and weak-crypto alerts are mostly false positives. No evidence found of prompt injection text or an unknown exfiltration endpoint, but the credential-handling guidance makes this unsafe to publish without remediation.

high_findings.title: Hidden Credential File Lookup Instruction
high_findings.description: The skill tells the agent that CONTEXT7_API_KEY is stored in hidden .env files and instructs it to search there. This is a real credential-access risk because marketplace skills should not direct agents to inspect hidden secret files, even when the named secret is service-specific.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly names a secret-bearing environment variable and hidden .env locations. The context confirms this is operational guidance, not incidental text.

high_findings.title: Authenticated External Requests With User Queries
high_findings.description: The Python helper sends an Authorization bearer token to the hardcoded Context7 API and includes user-supplied library and query parameters in outbound requests. This appears intended for documentation retrieval, but it still creates confirmed network and secret exposure risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly builds HTTPS requests to context7.com and attaches the API key as a bearer token. The endpoint is expected for this skill, so confidence is high for risk presence but not for malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Shell-Based Workflow Requires Executing Bundled Script
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs agents to run python3 commands from user home skill paths. The examples are normal usage for this skill, but any marketplace workflow that asks agents to execute bundled scripts increases review and maintenance risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippets are documented examples rather than dynamic command construction. Risk remains because the skill workflow depends on executing local code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms, but the referenced lines contain documentation text and argparse setup, not cryptographic functions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, or other weak crypto usage in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows no cryptographic API calls at the flagged locations. The alerts appear caused by keyword matches such as auth or context text.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are Mostly Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backtick spans as command execution. Most occurrences are markdown examples or quick-reference commands, not code that executes automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside markdown code fences or table examples. It can influence agent behavior, but it is not Ruby or runtime backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Discovery Guidance In Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instruction tells agents to search hidden .env files for an API key. This pattern can normalize secret-file access and should be replaced with explicit user-provided environment setup.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording is direct and includes hidden file paths. The behavior is dangerous even if the intended credential is for Context7.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent To External Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The script constructs an Authorization bearer header and sends it in HTTPS requests to Context7. This is expected functionality, but it should be disclosed because it transmits credentials and user queries outside the local workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The request creation and URL construction are explicit. The hardcoded host limits ambiguity about destination, but the external transmission is confirmed.

content.user_title: Fetch Current Library Docs with Context7
content.value_statement: Library APIs change faster than model knowledge. This skill queries Context7 so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can reference current documentation during implementation.
actual_capabilities: Searches Context7 for libraries by name.
Fetches documentation context for a selected library ID.
Supports focused queries about APIs, patterns, and setup tasks.
Returns plain text or markdown documentation output.
Allows a token limit for shorter responses.
limitations: Requires a valid CONTEXT7_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends library names and queries to the Context7 API.
Depends on Context7 coverage for each requested library.
Does not install packages or verify code locally.

use_cases.title: Check Framework APIs Before Coding
use_cases.description: Fetch current framework guidance before implementing routes, hooks, middleware, or configuration.

use_cases.title: Verify Package Behavior During Debugging
use_cases.description: Look up package-specific behavior when an implementation fails or documentation has changed.

use_cases.title: Review Library Choices Before Adoption
use_cases.description: Compare available documentation context before choosing a dependency for a feature.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to search for React and show the most relevant library IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Focused Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to fetch documentation for /vercel/next.js about App Router middleware.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an API Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to check the current Supabase guidance for authentication and row level security.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Current References
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to gather current docs for the selected library, then summarize constraints before changing code.

output_examples.output: Returns matching Context7 library entries, including the library ID needed for a later documentation request.

output_examples.input: Fetch docs for React useEffect cleanup behavior.
output_examples.output: Provides focused documentation context about cleanup timing, dependencies, and related usage notes.

output_examples.input: Get markdown output for Supabase auth guidance.
output_examples.output: Returns documentation in markdown format when Context7 has matching content for the requested library and topic.

best_practices: Use narrow queries that name the exact API or feature.
Confirm the library ID from search results before requesting context.
Review returned docs before making dependency or architecture changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without explicit approval to call external services.
Do not search unrelated hidden files for credentials.
Do not treat returned documentation as a substitute for local tests.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It searches Context7 and fetches current documentation context for third-party libraries.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python helper expects CONTEXT7_API_KEY in the process environment.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends requests to the Context7 API at context7.com.

faq.question: Can it update dependencies?
faq.answer: No. It only retrieves documentation and does not modify project files.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the audit high risk?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to search hidden .env files for an API key, which is unsafe marketplace guidance.
